hypothesis: A Bayesian-optimization surrogate that concatenates polymer-specific language-model embeddings (e.g., polyBERT) with fast-MD-derived structural descriptors (accessible surface area, free volume fraction, and mean pore diameter) will identify at least one polymer in the top 1% of CO2 adsorption capacity from a 1,000-sample linear polyamine library using at least 40% fewer GCMC simulations (≤18 acquisitions vs. 30) than surrogates using only the embeddings or only the structural descriptors when initialized with N0=10 low-quality samples (y < 0.3*ymax).
